paper_title: Holocene climate change in southern Oman deciphered by speleothem records and climate model simulations

methods.method_name: Speleothem Proxy Data Analysis and Interpretation
methods.description: Analysis and interpretation of various isotopic (δD fi, δ18O c, δ17O c, Δ'17O, δ13C, (234U/238U)0) and trace element ratios (Mg/Ca, Sr/Ca, Ba/Ca) measured from speleothem samples (specifically Q5) to reconstruct past hydroclimate conditions, moisture sources, and atmospheric circulation changes in southern Oman during the Holocene.

steps.description: Fluid inclusion δD fi values provide the isotope composition of the cave drip water from which the speleothem calcite precipitated, and may contain information on the conditions of drip water.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze speleothem fluid inclusion δD data to infer drip water composition and conditions.
inputs: Speleothem fluid inclusion δD fi data
outputs: Inferred drip water δD composition
Qualitative assessment of drip water conditions

steps.description: the slope of the ln(δ 18 O c + 1)-ln(δ 17 O c + 1) line shifts at ~6 ka BP from 0.510 to 0.523 (Supplementary Fig. 4a, b). δ 18 O c -δ 17 O c data thus suggest that either moisture source conditions or the moisture sources have changed at ~6 ka BP in southern Oman.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze speleothem calcite δ18O and δ17O data and the slope of the ln(δ18O c + 1)-ln(δ17O c + 1) relationship to infer changes in moisture source.
inputs: Speleothem calcite δ18O c data
Speleothem calcite δ17O c data
outputs: δ18O c time series
δ17O c time series
Slope of ln(δ18O c + 1)-ln(δ17O c + 1) relationship
Indication of changes in moisture source conditions or sources

steps.description: Δ' 17 O is defined as the deviation of triple oxygen isotope data from a reference relationship between speleothem δ 17 O c and δ 18 O c 52 (See supplementary text)
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate Δ'17O from speleothem δ17O c and δ18O c data using a reference relationship.
inputs: Speleothem calcite δ18O c data
Speleothem calcite δ17O c data
Reference relationship parameters for triple oxygen isotopes
outputs: Speleothem Δ'17O time series

steps.description: Speleothem Δ' 17 O is not sensitive to Rayleigh distillation from the ocean to the continent... Therefore, it reflects mainly the original Δ' 17 O signal of water vapor, which in turn allows a calculation of the relative humidity at the moisture source... In addition, other factors might also contribute to some extent to the Δ' 17 O variation, such as the mixing of water vapors..., re-evaporation of precipitation..., and convections.
steps.searchable_summary: Interpret speleothem Δ'17O values to infer relative humidity at the moisture source, considering other potential influences.
inputs: Speleothem Δ'17O time series
Understanding of factors affecting Δ'17O (Rayleigh distillation, vapor mixing, re-evaporation, convection)
outputs: Inferred relative humidity at the moisture source time series
Qualitative assessment of other influences on Δ'17O

steps.description: Understanding of Qunf δ 18 O c record δ 18 O c values of stalagmite Q5 show an abrupt decrease... followed by a relatively stable period... and then by a persistent increase... The long-term increasing trend of the Qunf δ 18 O c record from ~6 ka BP to the present may indicate an overall Indian summer monsoon (or summer monsoon circulation) waning process.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze long-term trends and abrupt shifts in the speleothem δ18O c record to understand monsoon circulation changes.
inputs: Speleothem calcite δ18O c time series
outputs: Identification of trends (e.g., increasing/decreasing)
Identification of abrupt shifts
Interpretation regarding large-scale monsoon circulation changes and local precipitation amount effect

steps.description: This may lead to increased water flow rates in Qunf Cave's epikarst, resulting in lower ( 234 U/ 238 U) 0 values, as ( 234 U/ 238 U) 0 is influenced by water flow rates and water-rock interaction times.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze speleothem initial (234U/238U) ratios to infer water flow rates and water-rock interaction times.
inputs: Speleothem (234U/238U)0 data
outputs: Inferred water flow rates
Inferred water-rock interaction times
Qualitative assessment of epikarst hydrological conditions

steps.description: Combining the... δ 13 C... records from Q5... enables us to reconstruct local precipitation minus evaporation (P-E) over the Holocene... slightly increased P-E could favor increased organic materials and reduced prior calcite precipitation (PCP), resulting in more negative δ 13 C values... Q5 δ 13 C values... suggesting slightly lower P-E (or effective wetness) in the early and middle Holocene compared to the late Holocene.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze speleothem δ13C values to infer local precipitation minus evaporation (P-E) and conditions affecting organic materials and prior calcite precipitation (PCP).
inputs: Speleothem δ13C data
outputs: Inferred local P-E conditions
Assessment of organic material influence
Assessment of PCP influence

steps.description: Trace element ratios (Mg/Ca, Sr/ Ca, and Ba/Ca) show a significant decreasing trend... followed by low and stable ratios after ~6 ka BP... PCP often occurs in the vadose zone... and increases with reduced effective infiltration, affecting trace element ratios... The slightly lower P-E condition at 8 K... suggests increased evaporation... promoting PCP and resulting in observed higher trace element ratios.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze speleothem trace element ratios (Mg/Ca, Sr/Ca, Ba/Ca) to infer effective infiltration and prior calcite precipitation (PCP).
inputs: Speleothem Mg/Ca data
Speleothem Sr/Ca data
Speleothem Ba/Ca data
outputs: Inferred effective infiltration rates
Assessment of PCP influence
Qualitative assessment of vadose zone hydrological conditions

steps.description: The Qunf multiproxy datasets... allow for a thorough comparison between observed... Holocene hydroclimate conditions... Combining the ( 234 U/ 238 U) 0 , δ 13 C, and trace element records from Q5... enables us to reconstruct local precipitation minus evaporation (P-E) over the Holocene.
steps.searchable_summary: Synthesize interpretations from multiple speleothem proxy records (isotopes and trace elements) to reconstruct local hydroclimate evolution.
inputs: Interpretations from δD fi analysis
Interpretations from δ18O c and δ17O c analysis
Interpretations from Δ'17O analysis
Interpretations from (234U/238U)0 analysis
Interpretations from δ13C analysis
Interpretations from Mg/Ca, Sr/Ca, Ba/Ca analysis
outputs: Integrated local hydroclimate reconstruction (e.g., P-E, effective wetness, hydrological conditions) over time

steps.description: Consistently, the δD fi data from Hoti Cave (northern Oman)... also show a significant change... Moreover, the Soreq and Jeita cave δ 18 O c records from the Levant are also sensitive to changes in δ 18 O of the Eastern Mediterranean surface seawater... Additionally, δD wax records from eastern Africa... also exhibit a trend similar to the Q5 δ 18 O c record... After ~6 ka BP, the North African summer monsoon fringe retreated... associated with the termination of the Mediterranean sapropel S1 period...
steps.searchable_summary: Compare Qunf speleothem records with other regional paleoclimate records and indicators to understand regional climate dynamics and monsoon systems.
inputs: Qunf speleothem proxy records (δ18O c, δD fi, etc.)
Regional paleoclimate records (e.g., Hoti Cave δ18O c, Levant speleothem δ18O c, East African δD wax)
Information on regional climate indicators (e.g., Mediterranean sapropel S1 timing)
outputs: Identification of similar/dissimilar trends across regions
Insights into large-scale atmospheric circulation (e.g., North African summer monsoon, Indian summer monsoon, Afro-Asian monsoon) behavior

methods.method_name: Climate Model Simulation and Proxy-Model Comparison
methods.description: Using a climate model (EC-Earth) to simulate past hydrological conditions during the Holocene (specifically comparing 8 ka BP and pre-industrial periods) and comparing the simulation results with speleothem proxy records to validate interpretations and understand regional atmospheric circulation.

steps.description: To further understand the hydroclimate conditions at Qunf Cave, we compared the EC-Earth simulated summer (JJA) hydrological conditions between 8 K (8 ka BP) and PI (pre-industrial period).
steps.searchable_summary: Run the EC-Earth climate model to simulate climate conditions for periods like 8 ka BP (8 K) and pre-industrial (PI).
inputs: EC-Earth model configuration
Boundary conditions for 8 K (8 ka BP)
Boundary conditions for PI (pre-industrial period)
outputs: Simulated climate variables (e.g., wind fields, humidity, precipitation, temperature) for 8 K and PI periods

steps.description: Our model simulations indicate that during the 8 K period, the North African summer monsoon-characterized by the southwesterly wind extending across the continent and reaching the southern Arabian Peninsula was considerably stronger (Fig. 3a).
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze simulated summer (JJA) wind fields from the climate model to assess monsoon strength and extent.
inputs: Simulated summer wind fields from EC-Earth model
outputs: Assessment of monsoon strength and spatial extent for simulated periods

steps.description: The model simulations reveal a ~6% increase in relative humidity at the equatorial Atlantic, Arabian Sea and Qunf Cave site from 8 K to PI (Fig. 3b).
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze simulated relative humidity at key locations (e.g., moisture source areas, cave site) during summer.
inputs: Simulated summer relative humidity fields from EC-Earth model
outputs: Simulated relative humidity values/fields for specified locations/regions and periods

steps.description: Notably, the summer precipitation amount at Qunf Cave was higher at 8 K.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze simulated summer precipitation amount at the cave site location.
inputs: Simulated summer precipitation fields from EC-Earth model
outputs: Simulated summer precipitation amount at the cave site for simulated periods

steps.description: However, the summer P-E appears to be slightly lower over a large part of the Arabian Peninsula, suggesting a strong evaporation condition (Fig. 3, Supplementary Fig. 7).
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze simulated summer precipitation minus evaporation (P-E) over the study region.
inputs: Simulated summer precipitation fields from EC-Earth model
Simulated summer evaporation fields from EC-Earth model
outputs: Simulated summer P-E values/fields for the region and periods

steps.description: slightly increased P-E could favor increased organic materials and reduced prior calcite precipitation (PCP), resulting in more negative δ 13 C values in the late Holocene [78][79][80] . Q5 δ 13 C values range from -6.4‰ to 1.1‰, with relatively stable values (~−1‰) from ~10.8 to 6 ka BP and a decreasing trend from~−1‰ to ~−5‰ since ~6 ka BP (Fig. 2), suggesting slightly lower P-E (or effective wetness) in the early and middle Holocene compared to the late Holocene.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze simulated summer temperature at the cave site location.
inputs: Simulated summer temperature fields from EC-Earth model
outputs: Simulated summer temperature at the cave site for simulated periods

steps.description: The direction of these Holocene trends is consistent. Taking into account the uncertainties of the modeled and reconstructed relative humidity, these datasets show strong similarities... Both model simulation and proxy records show a similar increasing trend in local hydroclimate conditions at the cave site from 8 K to PI, the P-E was lower during the early to middle Holocene compared to the late Holocene... These results are consistent with Q5 δ 13 C and trace elements ratio data.
steps.searchable_summary: Compare trends and values from climate model simulations (e.g., relative humidity, P-E) with corresponding interpretations from speleothem proxy records.
inputs: Simulated climate variables (relative humidity, P-E, etc.) from EC-Earth model
Speleothem proxy interpretations (inferred relative humidity from Δ'17O, inferred P-E from δ13C/trace elements)
outputs: Assessment of consistency between model and proxy data
Validation of proxy interpretations
Insights into regional climate processes
